ddls:
CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistical model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model and its purpose */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the model */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the model was created */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., regression, classification) */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the model */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date the model was last modified */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the model is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model to which this parameter belongs */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., beta0, beta1) */,
  "mean" REAL /* Mean estimate of the parameter */,
  "sd" REAL /* Standard deviation of the parameter */,
  "lower_bound" REAL /* Lower bound of the credible interval */,
  "upper_bound" REAL /* Upper bound of the credible interval */,
  "parameter_type" TEXT /* Type of the parameter (e.g., intercept, slope) */,
  "is_significant" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is statistically significant */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the credible interval */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_parameters_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis result */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model analyzed */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the analysis was performed */,
  "result_summary" TEXT /* Summary of the analysis results */,
  "analysis_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the analysis */,
  "is_reproducible" INTEGER /* Indicates if the analysis can be reproduced */,
  "analysis_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the analysis in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_results_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model to which this metric belongs */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., accuracy, R-squared) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the performance metric (e.g., accuracy, precision, recall) */,
  "is_improved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the metric has improved since the last recording */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model for which the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model on which the action was performed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., created, modified) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date of the action */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the action */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates if the action was successful */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_logs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analysis result from which the report is generated */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the report was created */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the report document */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, HTML) */,
  "is_shared" INTEGER /* Indicates if the report has been shared with other users */,
  "sharing_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was shared */,
  "sharing_users" TEXT /* List of users with whom the report was shared */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES analysis_results ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role's responsibilities */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the role has administrative privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was assigned to the user */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the role is the default role for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
